ALL SPECIALTIES / PHARMACY TECHNICIAN / MICHIGAN

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Michigan Pharmacy Technician Registration

Michigan Pharmacy Technician license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-18 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Pharmacy Technician applications in Michigan run through Michigan Board of Pharmacy. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official Transcripts - Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score Report - Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 10 to 12 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Michigan by submitting a new application to the Michigan Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Michigan-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 10 to 12 Weeks.

  1. STEP 1

    1. Meet Eligibility Requirements and Obtain National Certification

    Before applying for a Michigan state license, applicants must be at least 18 years old and hold current national certification from either the Pharmacy Technician Certification Board (PTCB) or the National Healthcareer Association (NHA) ExCPT. This is a mandatory prerequisite for Michigan licensure.

    Fee: $137.70 · Timeline: 10 to 12 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    2. Create a MiPLUS Account

    Visit the MiPLUS online licensing portal at aca-prod.accela.com/lara/Default.aspx. Click 'Register for an Account' and follow the prompts to create a new user account, then verify your email address as instructed.

    Fee: $137.70 · Timeline: 10 to 12 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    3. Complete the Online Application

    Log in to your newly created MiPLUS account. From the MiPLUS home page, select 'Professional Licensing' and then locate and complete the appropriate 'Pharmacy Technician Application' (or 'Licensure by Endorsement (Pharmacy Technician)' if applying by endorsement). Ensure all required fields are accurately filled out.

    Fee: $137.70 · Timeline: 10 to 12 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    4. Upload Supporting Documents

    As part of the online application process within MiPLUS, upload all necessary supporting documentation. This primarily includes proof of your current national certification from either PTCB or NHA ExCPT.

    Fee: $137.70 · Timeline: 10 to 12 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    5. Pay the Application Fee

    Submit the non-refundable application fee of $25.00 through the MiPLUS portal using a credit card or electronic check. Your application will not be processed until the fee is received.

    Fee: $137.70 · Timeline: 10 to 12 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    6. Monitor Application Status

    Regularly check the status of your application by logging into your MiPLUS account. Respond promptly to any requests for additional information or documentation from the Bureau of Professional Licensing to avoid delays in processing.

    Fee: $137.70 · Timeline: 10 to 12 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$137.70
Background check
Fingerprint vendor fee paid separately
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$137.70 plus fingerprint vendor fees

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Renewal

Pharmacy Technician renewal in Michigan follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$61.20
Continuing education
See board

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Michigan pharmacy technician license cost?

Michigan lists a $137.70 pharmacy technician application and initial licensure fee, with a $61.20 renewal fee for the two-year cycle.

Is national certification required?

Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

Does Michigan have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Michigan. To work in Michigan, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Michigan?

Michigan’s current licensing report says pharmacy technician applications average about 90 days from receipt to issuance.

Can I start working in Michigan while my endorsement application is processing?

Contact the Michigan Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Michigan.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$137.70

Processing time

10 to 12 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

See board

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 2 years · $61.20

Licensing board

Michigan Board of Pharmacy
